concerns.text: Task T022 mandates implementing 'Hotelling's T-squared test', but the Plan (Constitution Check, Principle VII) and Constitution (Principle VII) explicitly require 'paired-sample t-tests (Bonferroni corrected)'. The task implements a multivariate test (Hotelling) which contradicts the constitutional requirement for univariate paired t-tests, violating the 'Statistical Rigor' constraint defined in the project's governing documents.

Task T020 only implements a 'config flag' for the Baseline MAML runner. It does not include the execution logic for the 5 independent training runs required by FR-004 and US-2 to generate the data vectors needed for the statistical comparison. Without a specific task to orchestrate the 5-seed baseline execution, the deliverable for FR-005 (statistical test) cannot be satisfied.

Task T027 hardcodes the sensitivity sweep parameters `[0.01, 0.05, 0.1]` in the task description. FR-006 and SC-002 require the system to 'accept a configurable list' of parameters. Hardcoding these values in the implementation task violates the requirement for configurability, preventing the system from adapting the sweep range if the default set proves inconclusive.

The spec's Edge Cases require handling 'covariance matrix singularity' for the statistical test if the baseline fails. Task T024 handles the 'undefined' verdict logic, but Task T022 (statistical analysis) lacks an explicit requirement to implement a regularization or fallback strategy for the covariance matrix inversion within the Hotelling's T-squared implementation. This leaves a critical failure mode (crash on singularity) unaddressed in the code logic.
